tool: fulcrum critpath differential kernel localizer (gz vs instrumented libdeflate 1.25)

semantic_alignment.table_load: litlen MAIN decode-table indexed load (subtable loads NOT bracketed, matching gz)
semantic_alignment.consume: litlen bit-consume (saved=bitbuf; bitbuf>>=; bitsleft-=) for main+subtable litlen entries; OFFSET consume NOT bracketed (matching gz)
semantic_alignment.dist_lookup: offset MAIN decode-table indexed load (offset subtable NOT bracketed)

primary_metric: tax-adjusted active-time per region (us). Criticality-weighting is CLEAN on gz (beta<1) but CONTAMINATED on libdeflate (beta>1.15, 'interference' on every region: injecting an independent ALU chain into libdeflate's fully software-pipelined C loop perturbs scheduling super-linearly), so active-time is the trusted differential.
fire_count_alignment_check: PASS — gz and ld fire each region the same count (<0.001% diff), an independent proof the regions are the same operations (counts are stream properties).
caveats: Walls are marker-instrumented (~57ms tax on each, equal & near-cancelling in the differential) AND gz runs its full parallel-SM T1 pipeline (scaffolding) vs libdeflate bare single-shot, so the ABSOLUTE wall gap (490 vs 289ms) overstates the real gz/libdeflate gap; the per-region KERNEL active-time differential is the valid comparison.

Only ~5% of each wall is inside the six bracketed ops (residual ~92-96% is loop control, table build, offset consume/subtable, crc, framing); match_copy is the largest LOCATED differential, not the whole gap.
libdeflate criticality slopes are interference-flagged; criticality-WEIGHTED wall_added is reported per-subject but the cross-decoder differential rests on active-time.
